title: Self-Debugging Success Rates
concept: Quantifying how compile-run-fix iterations drastically improve code correctness compared to single-shot generation.

exercises.prompt: When tracking coding mistakes, what helps prevent infinite loops in debugging?

options.text: Tracking step counts and diff history
Rewriting the entire code each time

exercises.explain: Tracking step counts and the history of changes helps ensure that the same mistake isn't repeated endlessly.

cards.title: Understanding Success Rates
cards.body: Self-debugging loops help us improve code correctness over time. Let's explore how repeated cycles of compiling, running, and fixing can dramatically boost our success rates!

cards.title: Success Rate
cards.body: The success rate is a measure of how often our code works correctly after each iteration of debugging and fixing.

cards.title: Like a Health Checkup
cards.body: Think of self-debugging as a doctor checking your health over time. Each visit (iteration) helps identify what needs fixing to keep you healthier (better code).

cards.title: Compile-Run-Fix Example
cards.body: If you run code and it fails, you find the error, fix it, and run it again. This cycle continues, gradually increasing the chances of success as mistakes are corrected.

cards.title: Myth: One-Time Fixes Are Enough
cards.body: Some believe fixing code just once is enough, but without further testing and fixing, errors can remain.

cards.title: Improvement Through Iteration
cards.body: Each iteration enhances our results, making code more reliable.

left.text: Only one attempt leads to many hidden errors.

right.text: Multiple attempts reveal and fix errors, leading to cleaner code.

exercises.prompt: What does a higher success rate in self-debugging indicate?
exercises.explain: A higher success rate means the code is more correct because it has been thoroughly tested and fixed multiple times.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Self-debugging leads to more errors in the code.
exercises.explain: False. Self-debugging helps to discover and rectify errors, thus reducing them.

exercises.prompt: By repeatedly compiling and running code, we can increase our __________ rates.
exercises.sentence: By repeatedly compiling and running code, we can increase our success rates.
exercises.explain: The correct term, 'success rates', reflects how often the code runs correctly after iterations.

exercises.prompt: Which statement about self-debugging is generated by AI?

options.text: Success rates improve with every iteration of testing and fixes.
Once code compiles correctly, no further checks are necessary.

exercises.explain: Correct! The AI-generated statement explains how iterative testing boosts success.

exercises.prompt: Why are compile-run-fix iterations more effective than single-shot generation in coding?

options.text: They allow for finding and fixing multiple errors at once.
They improve accuracy as mistakes get fixed with each attempt.
They require less time for code execution overall.
They eliminate the need for debugging entirely.

exercises.explain: Correct! Compile-run-fix iterations make it more likely to uncover and correct mistakes, leading to more accurate code.
